data.text: I’ll execute the bead directly. I’m going to inspect the app structure and existing UI/test patterns first, then add the primitives and focused smoke coverage before committing.

The worktree already has unrelated skill-directory changes, so I’ll leave those untouched. The first search showed no root `src/`; I’m narrowing to the frontend package and its existing Svelte/Vitest conventions.

Using the `frontend-design` skill because this bead adds reusable UI components. I’m reading its local guidance just far enough to align the component styling and verification with the repo’s frontend stack.

The frontend is SvelteKit under `cli/internal/server/frontend`, with bits-ui already installed. Unit tests currently run in a node Vitest project, so I’ll keep the smoke test lightweight and compatible with that setup unless the repo already has browser component test support elsewhere.

I need the bits-ui type surface to avoid guessing the Svelte 5 component API. I’m installing the frontend dependencies from the existing lockfile in the frontend package so `check` and unit tests can run locally.

I’ve got the component API shape now. I’m adding three standalone primitives under `src/lib/components`, plus a small smoke test that imports them through Vite and checks the typed-confirm gating helper.
The primitives are in place. I’m running Svelte checking and the targeted unit test now; if the bits-ui snippet or Svelte 5 typing needs adjustment, these should surface it quickly.

The components compile and the smoke test passes. Svelte flagged the focusable tooltip wrapper as an accessibility warning, so I’m tightening that wrapper to satisfy the disabled-button hover requirement without introducing a noninteractive tab stop.
The full frontend unit suite passes, and Svelte check has only two pre-existing warnings outside this bead. I’m formatting the new files, then I’ll rerun the focused checks before committing.

The new files are ignored by the repo’s broad `lib/` ignore rule, so I’ll force-add only these component files when staging. Before that I’m doing one build pass because `$lib/index.ts` now exports Svelte components and I want the package boundary checked.

Verification is complete for the touched frontend package: unit tests and build pass; full lint is blocked by existing formatting drift in unrelated frontend files, while the new files pass Prettier. I’m committing only the staged primitive files now.

Implemented and committed `ddx-84daf44b`.

Commit: `808ccfdd feat(ui): add shared dialog and tooltip primitives [ddx-84daf44b]`

Added:
- `ConfirmDialog.svelte`
- `TypedConfirmDialog.svelte`
- `Tooltip.svelte`
- `primitives.smoke.spec.ts`
- `$lib` exports in `src/lib/index.ts`

Verified:
- `bun run check` passed with only two existing unrelated warnings
- `bun run test:unit -- --run` passed: 3 files, 8 tests
- `bun run build` passed
- New files pass Prettier check

`bun run lint` is still blocked by pre-existing formatting drift in unrelated frontend files.
